model.label: Gemini 3 Flash
model.description: Google • Fast Gemini model optimized for low-latency reasoning, multimodal inputs, and tool use.

pick.rationale: Duke enters the national championship as the analytically superior team, boasting the nation's #1 adjusted defensive efficiency and a top-5 offense that provides an elite two-way balance. While Arizona is a formidable opponent at full strength with star freshman Koa Peat and a top-5 defense of their own, they face a significant challenge in penetrating a Blue Devils unit that has been historically dominant all season. Despite navigating the tournament without starting guard Caleb Foster and center Patrick Ngongba II, Duke's interior length—anchored by ACC Tournament MVP Cameron Boozer—and their prior experience defeating Arizona in the 2025 Sweet 16 give them the edge in this heavyweight title clash.

reasoningStep.title: National Championship Analysis: Duke vs. Arizona
reasoningStep.summary: Duke's historically high analytical profile and #1 ranked defense make them the favorites to secure the national title over a balanced and healthy Arizona squad.
evidence: Duke is ranked #1 in KenPom and Bart Torvik with the #1 adjusted defensive efficiency.
Arizona is ranked #3 in KenPom and Bart Torvik with the #3 adjusted defensive efficiency.
Duke defeated Arizona 100-93 in the 2025 Sweet 16.
Cameron Boozer was named ACC Tournament MVP; Koa Peat was named Big 12 Tournament MVP.
Duke advanced by defeating defending champion Florida in the Final Four.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.snippet: The Duke Blue Devils defeated the Virginia Cavaliers to win their second straight ACC Championship. Isaiah Evans led all scorers with 20 points, while Cayden Boozer tied his career-high with 16 points. result.title: Duke star Cameron Boozer dazzles to take down Clemson in ACC Tournament
result.snippet: The top-ranked Duke Blue Devils defeated the Clemson Tigers 73-61 in the ACC Tournament semifinal, led by a dominant performance from star guard Cameron Boozer . Boozer recorded a double-double with 24 points and 14 rebounds, while also contributing 5 assists. Duke controlled the game in the second half, outrebounding Clemson 41-27 and holding the Tigers to just 33% shooting overall.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: demo-2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: title
Current game label: National championship
Round: Championship
Resolved teams: Duke (duke) vs Arizona (arizona)
Prior committed picks: 66
